rounds.round1_standard: code quality / correctness / contract symmetry / build+tests

gates.go_python_naming_symmetry: PASS (%05s == zfill(5) across 0700/2800/9988/13/00001 incl edge cases)

findings.description: ^HSCE is NOT in indexMarkets, so MarketForSymbol(^HSCE) returns MarketUS (not MarketHK) and KnownIndexMarket(^HSCE)=not-known. Spec lines 85-88 explicitly mandate verifying AND adding ^HSI/^HSCE->HK classification. ^HSI was added, ^HSCE was missed. IMPACT IS BOUNDED: the export-ohlcv/qlib deliverable is UNAFFECTED (SelectForSymbol routes all ^-prefixed to yahoo regardless). The defect surfaces only in the in-app watchlist analysis: app.go:705 emits a misleading 'market defaults to US' warn for ^HSCE, and core.Fundamental.Market would be wrong IF fundamentals were built. ^HSCE is type=指数 with only price_percentile (no fundamentals), so no functional break today. Architect lens rated this CRITICAL; QA reconciled to WARNING on actual scope impact. Both lenses agree the defect EXISTS.
findings.fix: Add `"^HSCE": core.MarketHK,` to indexMarkets (selector.go:22) and a TestSelectForSymbol/MarketForSymbol case asserting ^HSCE->MarketHK. One-line fix, satisfies spec MUST-verify item.

findings.description: is_index checks c[2:5] in (000,399) for ALL instruments including HK (HKxxxxx). A low-numbered HK stock collides: 0001.HK->HK00001->c[2:5]=000 and 0013.HK->HK00013->c[2:5]=000 would be WRONGLY classified as index and pulled into the index correlation matrix. 0001.HK (长江和记) / 0013.HK (长实) are real HK blue chips. All 9 CURRENT watchlist HK codes are safe (verified), so this is latent, not triggered today — but bites on the next sub-100 HK code added.
findings.fix: Exclude HK instruments before the numeric segment check, e.g. `if c.startswith('HK'): return False` (HK indexes are explicitly HSI/HSCEI, not HKxxxxx).

findings.description: --market accepts any string; benchmarkForMarket/inMarket treat anything != 'hk' as cn. `--market us` or a typo `--market hkk` silently exports A-share data with the cn benchmark instead of erroring. Footgun for a CLI that drives data-bundle builds.
findings.fix: Validate in runExportOHLCV before resolve: reject market not in {cn,hk} with a clear error.

findings.description: qlib-data-hk invokes build_data.py WITHOUT --expected-symbols, while the cn target passes it. Loses the residual/stale-CSV validation guard for the HK bundle. Non-functional (build still succeeds).
findings.fix: Pass --expected-symbols for the HK set, or document why it is intentionally omitted.

findings.description: Market selection is magic-string `== "hk"` with implicit cn fallback in two functions. Adding a 3rd market is an O(n) shotgun edit. Acceptable design debt for a 2-market system; flag for future.
findings.fix: Optional: a small marketConfig registry {id,benchmark,inMarket} keyed by market id; also enables F3 validation centrally.

findings.description: QLIB_CSV_DIR (cn) lacks the _cn suffix that QLIB_CSV_HK_DIR has; analyze_watchlist default --qlib-dir is hardcoded to atlas_cn. HK runs must pass --qlib-dir explicitly. Usability nit, isolation itself is correct (separate target-dirs atlas_cn vs atlas_hk verified).
findings.fix: Optional: symmetric naming + error message mentioning both make targets.

verified_correct: Go/Python toQlibInstrument byte-symmetric for .HK/^HSI/^HSCE and rejection set (^HSTECH, 0700.HK.X, AAPL, ^GSPC, GC=F, BTC-USDT)
executeExportOHLCV uses p.Benchmark (no hardcoded 000300.SH); HK ^HSI fetch failure is fatal (TestExportOHLCV_HKBenchmarkFailureIsFatal)
resolveOHLCVSymbols(hk) selects .HK + ^HSI/^HSCE, excludes A-share, includes benchmark, de-dups order-preserved
requireBenchmark resolves per-market benchmark (TestRequireBenchmark_Market cn/hk x present/missing)
cn default zero-regression: TestResolveOHLCVSymbols_Default unchanged behavior
Market isolation: qlib-data->atlas_cn / qlib-data-hk->atlas_hk, separate csv+target dirs, no sharing
CSV filename derivation lowercase: HK00700->hk00700.csv, HSI->hsi.csv, HSCEI->hscei.csv matches spec contract table
watchlist: 4 ETF (2800/2828/3033/3181.HK) + 2 index (^HSI/^HSCE) added, all type-appropriate with ONLY price_percentile; ^HSTECH correctly excluded; 5 existing HK stocks unchanged
verdict_rationale: No CRITICAL with reviewer consensus; the export/qlib deliverable passes all gates. But three WARNING-level defects exist (F1 spec-mandated routing miss, F2 latent is_index HK collision, F3 unvalidated --market), and the two adversarial lenses disagreed on F1 severity (Architect=CRITICAL fix-before-merge, QA=WARNING bounded-impact). Above severity_threshold=warning, so not a clean PASS; needs Leader judgment on fix-before-accept vs accept-with-followup.
